rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY VS. CURTIS KEKOA JR. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defts To Serve Further Responses And Responses To Documents Production Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 6, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defts To Serve Further Responses And Responses To Documents Production Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions Denied. Moving party failed to meaningfully meet and confer prior to the filing of the instant motion. (CCP § 2016.040; see also Volkswagenwerk Aktiengesellschaft v. Sup.Ct. (1981) 122 Cal.App.3d 326.) Monetary sanctions are awarded in favor of Mr. Kekoa and against Plaintiff's counsel personally in the amount of $ 750.00 to be paid within 30 days of this order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party¿s counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: MUKUL BAKSHI VS. WORLD AFFAIRS COUNCIL OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 6, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT WORLD AFFAIRS COUNCIL OF NORTHERN CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Granted. Defendant has met its burden to show that there are no triable issues of fact as to claims for intentional infliction of emotional distress and negligence. Defendant's conduct was not extreme and outrageous. Mr. Ahsan's actions towards Plaintiff were not foreseeable. In opposition, Plaintiff has not met his shifting burden to show that there are triable issues of fact. To the extent that Plaintiff requests a denial, Plaintiff has not sufficiently demonstrated that Defendant engaged in abuse of discovery. (CCP § 437c(h); see Bothwell Decl., ¶ 6.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party¿s counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL WONG et al VS. THE OLYMPIC CLUB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 6, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANTS THE OLYMPIC CLUB, PAUL JACQUES, GREG DEROSA'S DEMURRER TO 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendants¿ demurrer is overruled in part and sustained in part. Defendants¿ demurrer is overruled as to the fourth and sixth causes of action. The Court has already held that Plaintiffs have alleged sufficient facts to state a claim. Demurring to the causes of action pursuant to CRC 2.112 is not a proper basis. (See CCP Section 430.10.) To the extent this is a motion for reconsideration, it is denied as the motion does not comply with CCP Section 1008. Defendant DeRosa¿s demurrer to the eighth cause of action is overruled as to Plaintiff Wong and sustained without leave to amend as to Plaintiff Canion. Plaintiff Wong has sufficiently pled facts that Defendant DeRosa¿s alleged harassment was motivated by Plaintiff Wong¿s age. (See Fourth Amended Complaint para 94.) Plaintiffs have not alleged facts that DeRosa¿s alleged harassment towards Plaintiff Canion was motivated by her protected status. Defendant Jacques demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to Plaintiffs Wong and Canion, as Plaintiffs have not alleged facts that Jacques¿ alleged harassment was motivated by Plaintiffs¿ protected status. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party¿s counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: EDWARD ROTSTEIN et al VS. TERRAFUSION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 6, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiffs EDWARD ROTSTEIN'S and TERRAFUSION INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S Motion to Compel Responses to Discovery Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THE MOTION OF PLAINTIFFS AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS EDWARD ROTSTEIN AND TERRAFUSION INTERNATIONAL, INC. TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES ¿ SET ONE IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (1) THE OBJECTIONS OF DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT TERRAFUSION, INC TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 2,3,5,6,7,8,9,10,13,14,15,16,17,20,21,22,23,24,25&26 ARE OVERRULED AND DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT TERRAFUSION WILL PROVIDE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES WITH 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT OBJECTIONS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 18&19 ARE SUSTAINED. (2) THE OBJECTIONS OF DEFENDANT AND CROSS-C0MPLAINANT TERRAFUSION, INC TO PLAINTIFFS AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS EDWARD ROTSTEIN AND TERRAFUSION INTERNATIONAL¿S REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS NOS 2,3,4,5,6,7,8,10,12,17,18,19,20,21,22,23&24. DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT TERRAFUSION, INC. WILL PRODUCE THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS WITHIN 30 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. TO THE EXTENT THAT ANY DOCUMENTS ARE WITHHELD ON THE GROUNDS OF ATTORNEY/CLIENT PRIVILEGE OR A CLAIM THAT THE DOCUMENTS CONTAIN TRADE SECRETS, DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT TERRAFUSION WILL PROVIDE TO PLAINTIFFS AND CROSS-DEFENDANTS EDWARD ROTSTEIN AND TERRAFUSION INTERNATIONAL, INC A LOG OF SUCH DOCUMENTS WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ALLOW A MOTION TO BE MADE ATTACKING THE CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT TERRAFUSION MAY HAVE HAD SOME LEGITIMATE BASIS FOR SOME OF ITS OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUESTED DISCOVERY SUCH AS LIMITATION AS TO TIME THE USE OF BOILERPLATE OBJECTIONS AND THE FAILURE TO PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS IN RESPONSE TO THE VAST MAJORITY OF THE REQUESTS OR RESPOND TO THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES DOES NOT DEMONSTRATE A GOOD FAITH ATTEMPT TO COMPLY WITH ITS DISCOVERY OBLIGATIONS. MONETARY SANCTIONS WILL BE IMPOSED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1500.00 COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY SHOULD PREPARE AND APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT IT TO COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 6, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Full And Complete Responses To Request For Inspection And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 6, 2013, Line 2: Defendants MOHAMED ELHILAA'S and MARTIN SMITH'S Motion to Compel Plaintiff to Provide Full and Complete Responses to Request for Inspection and Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS MOHAMED ELHILAA AND MARTIN SMITH¿S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS NOS. 10,11,13,14,16,17.18 & 19 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF NICOLE DEMEO¿S OBJECTIONS TO SUCH REQUESTS ARE WITHOUT MERIT. TO THE EXTENT THAT PLAINTIFF NICOLE DEMEO BELIEVES THAT ANY OF THE MATERIALS REQUESTED CONTAINS SENSITIVE INFORMATION OF A THIRD PARTY AN APPROPRIATE PROTECTIVE ORDER CAN BE FASHIONED UPON A SHOWING THAT SUCH INFORMATION IS ENTITLED TO CONFIDENTIALITY. NO MONETARY SANCTIONS WERE REQUESTED AND NONE WILL BE AWARDED. COUNSEL FOR THE MOVING PARTY SHOULD PREPARE AND APPROPRIATE ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT AT THE HEARING ON SEPTEMBER 6, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. LUCIE KELLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, September 6, 2013, Line 24, DEFENDANT LUCIE KELLY'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Denied. Statutory provisions are to be liberally construed to effectuate service if actual notice has been received by defendant. (See Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1387.) Plaintiff met the requirements of CCP §415.20(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
